name: paprika-proxy-fetch

get_cookies.description: Proxied HTTP GET for hosts that block paprika's egress IP. Sends a browser-shaped GET through a configured HTTP/SOCKS5 proxy and returns whatever cookies the response sets, plus a short body excerpt for diagnosis. Strategy 4 in the Cloudflare bake-off: complements paprika-flare (which handles JS challenges but cannot bypass an outright IP ban). Use when HostKnowledge.per_page.barriers.cloudflare_challenge.subtype = 'ip_banned'.

input_schema.proxy: string?  (HTTP(S) or SOCKS5 URL; falls back to env PAPRIKA_PROXY_URL)
input_schema.headers: dict?    (extra headers merged over the default browser-shape)

notes: Paired with paprika-flare. Where paprika-flare uses a Worker Chrome to clear JS challenges from inside the LAN (cookies are IP-matched to the Worker NAT pool), paprika-proxy-fetch routes the request through a clean external proxy so it bypasses IP-level blocks (CF error 1020 / region geo-fence). The proxy URL is supplied per-call (HostKnowledge.barriers.*.tool_params.proxy) or via PAPRIKA_PROXY_URL env. v0.1 does NOT auto-rotate -- operator points it at a single residential / datacenter proxy; rotation will be added once we have a pool to feed it.
